meaning: n.打碎的物体,破碎器,碎石槌
examples: Her flint-knapper today is Dennis Sandgathe, an archaeologist from Simon Fraser University in Canada.
It took millions of years of evolution to produce the hands of a skilled flint-knapper like Sandgathe.
Simply watching a practised knapper at work shows just how many skills the maker of our hand axe must have possessed.
